metadata.name: BGP Route Hijack — Cloudflare 1.1.1.1 (June 2024)
metadata.description: Reconstructs the Cloudflare 1.1.1.1 BGP hijack. AS267613 announces unauthorized /32 route, Tier-1 provider accepts as blackhole, route leak propagates across 300+ networks in 70 countries. Mapped to network infrastructure policy changes Guardian would evaluate.
metadata.author: Guardian Security Team

events.description: Normal BGP announcement update — routine prefix change

payload.business_context: Routine BGP prefix update for 1.1.1.0/24. Origin AS13335. RPKI valid.

events.description: AS267613 announces unauthorized 1.1.1.1/32 — more specific than Cloudflare's /24

payload.business_context: Unauthorized BGP announcement: 1.1.1.1/32 from AS267613 (Eletronet, Brazil). RPKI INVALID — ROA specifies AS13335 max /24. Longest-prefix-match causes global traffic redirection.

events.description: Tier-1 provider accepts /32 as RTBH blackhole route — traffic dropped

payload.business_context: Tier-1 accepts RPKI-invalid route as RTBH blackhole via AS-SET matching. All traffic to 1.1.1.1 silently dropped.

events.description: Route leak: AS262504 leaks 1.1.1.0/24 upstream to IX — 300 networks affected

payload.business_context: Route leak: customer prefix 1.1.1.0/24 re-announced to provider AS1031. Propagated to 300+ networks across 70 countries. 50-400ms latency increase.

events.description: Cloudflare BMP monitoring detects rogue /32 on route servers

payload.business_context: Internal BMP detection: rogue 1.1.1.1/32 seen on route servers. RPKI invalid. DFZ invalid. Auto-rejected by Cloudflare routers.

events.description: Cloudflare disables peering with AS267613 — first mitigation

payload.business_context: Incident response: disable BGP peering session with AS267613 (Eletronet). Immediate mitigation for hijack.
Disable second BGP peering with AS267613 for non-Brazilian traffic.

events.description: Route leak fully resolved — AS262504 ceases unauthorized announcements

payload.business_context: Full service recovery. All BGP monitoring returns to clean state. Total incident duration: ~7.5 hours.
